sentence_text: I can't concentrate at all now.

sentence_text: I've come out of a major depressive episode that lasted about a year and I still have some problems with focus.

Although concentration problems are widespread in depression, and in this person it seems to be a specific episode, this can be a risk factor if the concentration problem becomes widespread and affects other areas of life without there being a situation that causes it.

sentence_text: When I was forced to make decisions I had such anxiety before during and after.
explanation: In this post you can see an indecisive character to make decisions, people with anxiety and depression can have this pattern of behavior. If this indecision generates a sadness for the consequences caused by not taking them at the right time, it can possibly trigger symptoms related with problems  to make decisions and difficulty thinking as described in the seventh diagnostic criterion of major depression of the DSM-5.

sentence_text: I'm fighting with myself to not make a stupid decision, and it's tearing me apart.
explanation: According to this post, this person is overwhelmed by feelings of indecision and doubt about making decisions. These symptoms are recurrent in people with depressive symptomatology. If these feelings are recurrent and clinically significant could be depressive symptoms. In this context, is important to pay attention to this kind of emotional problem because it can last over time and facilitate the appearance of other cognitive distortions.

sentence_text: I can't concentrate, I don't care about getting good grades, I don't go to class b/c I can't get out of bed, I feel sick when I get a bad grade or don't know something, I feel alone in the classroom, or i won't be able to complete a class so then i start thinking about my future and how i won't graduate because i can't complete the coursework, so i won't get a job, won't get a house, be broke, no one will want to marry me, can't provide for family, friends hate me, etc, the list goes and on and on it goes.

sentence_text: Where in the past I could easily study for hours without getting distracted, now I cannot effectively study more than a few minutes per day.
explanation: The comment "Where in the past I could easily study for hours without getting distracted, now I cannot effectively study more than a few minutes per day" may indicate difficulty concentrating, which is a key symptom of major depressive disorder, according to the DSM-5. If this concentration difficulty persists and is accompanied by other symptoms, it could be a manifestation of a depressive episode.
